TRAITS
Mercenaries (pp 135-159}. Rules regarding BattleMech and vehicle repair and retrofitting can be found in the BattleTech Master Rules (pp. 82-881.
There are technological devices that can aid 0r cure this condition: If such device is permanently installed (such as bionic replacement). the trait is worth no points t0 the character
PAIN RESISTANCE Cost: 3 character with this trait has exceptional resistance pain: Characters with Pain Resistance are not stunned Grazing Wounds and the total TN modifiers for all wounds are reduced by For example character with Pain Resistance Kho has suffered two Minor Wounds and Serious Wound would have total TN modifier of +3, rather than +4_
POVERTY Value: character with this trait poor. has valuable assets and has only 100 C-bills to purchase starting equip ment: This trait may not be taken combination with Well- Equipped _ Land Grant or Owns Vehicle.
PROMOTION Cost: This is not actually trait; but It IS how an increase in the Rank Trait is listed in the Life Path_ Each Promotion adds the Ievel of the character'5 Rank Trait; t0 maximum of 10. number in parentheses adds the number t0 the level ot the character'  Rank Trait, to maximum of 10.
POISON RESISTANCE Cost: The character has developed an exceptional resistance to poison , including alcohol and other intoxicants Most often use barroom drinking contests this also helps the character resist the kind of drugs used by certain interrogators in the Capellan Confederation any cases where the gamemaster feels this trait would help the character receives 2 Modifier to appropriate target numbers
QUIRK (multiple trait) Value: 1-2 The character has code of behavior mental disorder that affects his conduct in certain situations. This trait iS defined by the player and can range from personal code of honor to deeply held religious beliefs to intense phobias; Tnis trait can represent any kind of mental or emotiona stricture that severe enough t0 force the character to act in certain ways even when might not be in the character' best interests_ The point value of Quirk is based 0n its severity or the character" commitment to it (in the case of vows Or codes): 2-point Quirk very deeply held _ and the character will never under any circumstances intentionally violate it. 1-point Quirk is serious _ but the character can force himself to set aside with successful WIL Check at cost of Fatigue If the Quirk is less severe than this Ie . no WIL Check would be required to bypass it), i5 t0o minor t0 be worth Character Points.
POOR HEARING Value: 1-5 The character has bad hearing and  cannot make oul sounds as well as most people_ Each point of Poor Hearing adds Modifier t0 all hearing-based target numbers Poor Hearing rated at points represents total deafness, making all hearing based Action Checks impossible. There are technological devices that can aid Or cure this condition_ It such device is permanently installed (such as bionic replacement), the trait wortn points (ne character:
POOR VISION value: 2/4/6 The character has bad vision and cannot see as weil as most people. The more points in the trait, the worse tne char- acter vision: for points of Poor Vision the character suffers +1 target number modifier for all sight-based Action Checks lincluding ranged attacks), though this can be corrected with glasses or contact lenses points represents very poor vision resulting +3 t0 all signt-based TNs that iS reduced to +1 the character wears corrective lenses. Poor Vision rated at 6 points represents blindness, making impossible for the character to make ranged attacks against targets fartner than Short Range and applying Ine Terrible Visibility Modifier (+51 to all sight-based TNs (including attacks) regardless of the actual lighting conditions Because blind char- acters rely on their hearing they also have the Poor Hearing Trait ; iney must apply the Poor Hearing modifiers ranged attack rolls. character who is both blind and deaf (6 points of Poor Vision and points of Poor Hearing) cannot make ranged attacks at all;
RANK Cost: 1-10 This trait represents tne character 5 military rank; though there are distinctions in authority similar t0 rank in civilian orga nizations these structures are not normally as organized and codified as the rank structure this trait represents; Non military characters ranks should be determined by the gamemaster based their SOC Attribute and other relevant factors_ For information regarding military structure and rank tne BattleTech universe_ see War and Its Makers (p. 1941 and The Clans (p 1891. Rank increases are listed in the Life Path as the Promotion Trait; while Rank decreases are listed as the Demotion Trait. Characters need the Commission Trait (see 801 t0 become an officer. Otherwise it is assumed every character is noncom- missioned oificer (NCO), otherwise known as an enlisted soldier: The more points of Rank character has, the higher rank he has Cross-reterence the trait points with the character" sta
